domain: Snowshoe hare seasonal coat-color swap — same animal, two coat phenotypes (summer brown, winter white) triggered by photoperiod → ASIP/agouti switch; 10-week gradual molt.
specific_mechanism: External photoperiod sensor + endogenous gradual phenotype swap; same individual hosts both phenotypes via switchable expression.
llm_application: SEASONAL-LORA: same base model + TWO pre-trained LoRA adapter sets per task domain. External context-sensor (latency budget, query difficulty, throughput pressure) triggers a GRADUAL 10-step bias-blend swap between the two adapter sets. Differs from instant LoRA hot-swap, from MoE per-token routing, and from single fine-tuning by maintaining BOTH phenotypes simultaneously with biologically-inspired gradual swap.
novelty_claim: LoRA hot-swap is instant. MoE routes per token. Standard fine-tuning produces one model. SEASONAL-LORA's distinctive contribution is MAINTAINING two simultaneous adapter sets per task with EXTERNAL-CUE-driven GRADUAL multi-step bias-blend — phenotypic alternation rather than instant swap.
